summary: One agent per judged-wrong row: root-cause every reader miss against the real store, cluster, verify, rank fixes

taxonomy.name: Silent export truncation (packed[:max_sources] drops store records unseen)
taxonomy.expected_point_yield: +10-14 rows on LME-S whole-export-freetier (16 unique questions, all in_store=true; highest-confidence cluster in the set — one mechanical bug in pack_record_sources explains every member; residual 2-6 rows still depend on the reader finding the now-present fact, though 9/16 are iter=yes/likely, i.e. rank-1-retrievable once exported)
taxonomy.fix: Make every bounded export lossless-or-loud: size the per-source char budget from the data (max_chars = ceil(total_chars / max_sources)) and bin-pack ALL records into exactly max_sources sources (splitting oversized records into chunks) instead of positional [:N] truncation; when a hard platform cap genuinely cannot hold the store, degrade by relevance-ranked or newest-valid_at-first packing so the dropped tail is the least-critical evidence, never the newest supersession-critical facts. Enforce with a post-export coverage invariant — every store record's content hash must appear in exactly one exported source — that fails the run loudly and emits records_exported/records_total in the run manifest, so a capacity overflow can never masquerade as a memory miss.

taxonomy.name: Judge and gold-label miscalibration (correct-or-defensible answers scored wrong)
taxonomy.expected_point_yield: Reclassifies ~11-14 of 14 unique LoCoMo rows (18 rows incl. duplicates, ~28-35% of a 40-question window) plus 1 LME-S preference row — but this is judge calibration / measurement honesty, NOT a memory-system improvement; 2-3 members (c9_q137, c7_q116) are outright dataset label noise (gold's evidence turn spoken by a different person) that no model fix can or should recover
taxonomy.fix: Two-sided scoring hardening, no retrieval changes: (1) judge side — deterministic gold-coverage pre-check before any LLM call; item-level coverage scoring for list/enumeration golds (split gold on commas/conjunctions, require each item entailed, never penalize store-grounded extras); normalize both answers to a minimal direct-answer span (date/entity/number, duration-tolerant for temporal golds) before comparing; majority-vote k=3 on remaining borderline LLM judgments. (2) dataset side — an automated gold-integrity pre-audit that cross-checks the question's named subject against the speaker field of the annotated evidence turn and quarantines mismatched rows into a separately-scored annotation-defect bucket. Complementary reader rule that also generalizes: always lead with the one-line direct answer (verdict token for polar questions), placing premise corrections and hedges after it, so strict same-answer judges grade the headline not the caveat.

taxonomy.name: Temporal anchor corruption (naive-local parse -> UTC render shifts calendar days; relative dates unresolved)
taxonomy.expected_point_yield: +2-3 rows on LoCoMo temporal (3 unique: c0_q49, c7_q22, c6_q29) and +1-2 rows on LME-S temporal-reasoning (gpt4_74aed68e, gpt4_2f8be40d); high confidence — the tz round-trip is a deterministic code bug with a testable invariant, the anchor-injection half is a one-line prompt change
taxonomy.fix: Keep one wall-clock convention end-to-end and never make the reader do date arithmetic: at ingest, parse source timestamps timezone-aware (or preserve the verbatim source date string in metadata) and make every export/render path emit valid_at in that same original frame — add a round-trip invariant test (parse -> store -> render must reproduce the source's calendar day). At export/read time, stamp each record's event date visibly in its header, pre-resolve relative temporal expressions ("last year", "day after tomorrow") against the record's valid_at into normalized absolute dates, and inject an explicit NOW anchor (query as_of, else max valid_at of the read set) with an instruction to resolve all deictic time expressions against that anchor, never the run-time wall clock.

taxonomy.name: Missing verification loop around the black-box reader (premises, supersession, denials unchecked)
taxonomy.expected_point_yield: +2-3 rows on LME-S whole-export-freetier (3 unique questions: false-premise trap, knowledge-update conflict, reader false-negative denial; all facts in store and exported)
taxonomy.fix: Wrap any external/black-box reader in a store-aware verification loop: (1) before synthesis, extract the question's presuppositions and verify each against the claim graph / value_as_of, forcing a premise-correction-first answer when a presupposition is contradicted by a current-value claim; (2) pre-resolve fact-slot conflicts at export by running supersession/truth-ledger logic into a dated "current truth (latest statement wins)" digest source, since an undated-chunk reader cannot infer recency; (3) treat a reader-returned denial/insufficiency as a re-ask trigger — run own top-k retrieval, pin the top source into a grounded re-ask or fall back to the internal verify-or-abstain reader before accepting "no information".

taxonomy.name: Enumeration/aggregation questions answered from top-k relevance instead of a complete candidate set
taxonomy.expected_point_yield: +1-2 rows (1 LoCoMo multi-hop on whole-export-freetier: c5_q2; 1 LME-S temporal-reasoning count question on retrieval-guided: a3838d2b) — small n but the failure mode is systematic: confident-wrong set answers can never self-trigger widening, so the set must be complete on round 1
taxonomy.fix: Detect enumeration/count/list intent ("how many", "what kinds of X has Y done", "list all") and switch read-set construction from relevance top-k to completeness mode: deterministically union ALL records whose extracted claims match the set-defining predicate (participant + activity/category edges in the claim graph, independent of lexical overlap with the question) into an explicit candidate checklist handed to the reader ("verify/include or reject each"), and run a post-draft completeness sweep for additional category instances the first pass missed. Requires typed participant-activity claim extraction at ingest so the rollup exists to union over.

taxonomy.name: Retrieval-guided read-set construction blind spots (head-truncated rerank, undecomposed multi-entity queries)
taxonomy.expected_point_yield: +1-2 rows on LME-S retrieval-guided (2 unique questions, both iter=yes — the shipped iterative_recall loop already recovers them reactively, so the proactive fix mainly saves a round trip and hardens against confident-wrong first drafts)
taxonomy.fix: Make read-set construction query-aware end-to-end: (1) in reranking, score any document beyond the cross-encoder's input window as the max over query-centered spans (multi-span max-pooling via the existing rerank_span_input path) so a candidate ranked #1 by dense and BM25 can never be demoted on text the reranker never saw; (2) for questions referencing two or more distinct entities, proactively decompose into per-entity attribute sub-queries and union each sub-query's top-k into the read set before the first read, since composite-question tokens dilute both BM25 and dense matching for every individual hop record.

taxonomy.name: Image-borne facts never textualized at ingest (no fix exists as-ingested)
taxonomy.expected_point_yield: +0-2 rows on LoCoMo multi-hop (2 unique questions) — honest ceiling: where the source ships only a generic caption and the image bytes/URL are unresolvable, NO retrieval, export, or reader fix can recover the fact (it never entered the store); the only honest handling for those rows is reclassification as unanswerable-as-ingested, and the +2 materializes only if image bytes or entity-bearing metadata (alt text, search query, filename) are actually available at ingest
taxonomy.fix: Entity-preserving multimodal ingest: when a memory carries an image reference, run vision at remember-time (OCR + fine-grained captioning + title/cover recognition) on the actual bytes rather than trusting a generic caption, preserve ALL source-provided image metadata (URL, alt text, search query, filename) as searchable record text, and attach extracted entities to the deictic utterance that referenced the image ("how about this series?" + photo). Where vision is unavailable or the image is unresolvable, store an explicit "unresolved visual referent" lacuna so the reader can surface the gap honestly — and the eval harness should flag rows whose gold depends on un-ingested modalities as unanswerable-as-ingested rather than scoring them as memory misses.

The '+2-3 LoCoMo temporal rows' expected_point_yield is therefore overstated: 2 of 2 sampled members would not convert.
